metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define AC3's proving command: name the specific artifact (e.g. `.coveragerc`, `coverage.xml`, a coverage-inventory doc) and provide a concrete command (e.g. `grep -c 'test_verify_command_lint_documents_shellcheck_prior_art' coverage-inventory.md` returning 0) that produces evidence of non-inclusion.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Neither is counted as behavioral coverage in any coverage inventory') names no concrete proving command and does not identify what artifact or system constitutes the 'coverage inventory' — an executor cannot verify this criterion without knowing where to look or what command to run.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC3 with: 'Neither test name appears in `coverage-inventory.md` (or equivalent); verify with `grep -c test_verify_command_lint_documents_shellcheck_prior_art coverage-inventory.md` and `grep -c test_no_total_runtime_timeout_mechanism coverage-inventory.md`, both returning 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC2 to explicitly state the disposition of the `signal.alarm`/`Timer(` absence assertions (e.g., 'retained as-is' if they are behaviorally anchored, or 'also dropped/loosened' if they are equally brittle).
findings.finding: AC2 addresses only the `asyncio.run(...)` exact-string assertion but leaves unresolved the fate of the `signal.alarm`/`Timer(` absence checks that the Why section also identifies as part of the same brittle test.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC2 to: 'The no-total-timeout test drops the exact `asyncio.run(...)` string assertion AND clarifies whether the `signal.alarm`/`Timer(` absence checks are retained (as acceptable structural guards) or also removed; the liveness property is anchored on the behavioral timeout probes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC2 to explicitly cover all three source-string assertions in `test_no_total_runtime_timeout_mechanism`: state whether `assert 'signal.alarm' not in src` and `assert 'Timer(' not in src` (lines 326-327) should also be dropped or retained with documented rationale.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section identifies all three source-string assertions in `test_no_total_runtime_timeout_mechanism` as brittle (`signal.alarm` absent, `Timer(` absent, and `asyncio.run(...)` present), but AC2 only mandates dropping 'the exact `asyncio.run(...)` string assertion'. The two negative-space assertions at lines 326–327 (`assert 'signal.alarm' not in src` and `assert 'Timer(' not in src`) have no disposal specified in any AC, leaving them intact after a literal AC2 implementation and defeating the plan's stated goal.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC2 to: 'The no-total-timeout test drops all source-string assertions (`signal.alarm` not in src, `Timer(` not in src, and the exact `asyncio.run(...)` string check); the liveness property is anchored on the behavioral timeout probes alone.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC2 whether `assert "signal.alarm" not in src` and `assert "Timer(" not in src` (test_llm_timeouts.py:326-327) are also in scope for removal or relabeling, alongside the `asyncio.run(...)` assertion.
findings.finding: The plan says the no-total-timeout test 'drops the exact asyncio.run(...) string assertion' but is silent on whether the two other source-text guards in the same test — `assert "signal.alarm" not in src` and `assert "Timer(" not in src` (lines 326-327) — should also be dropped, kept, or relabeled. Those are equally source-string structural assertions with no runtime behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2 to read: 'The no-total-timeout test drops or relabels all three source-text string assertions (signal.alarm, Timer(, asyncio.run(...)); the liveness property is anchored on the behavioral timeout probes only.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC2 to explicitly address all three source-string assertions in `test_no_total_runtime_timeout_mechanism` — state whether `assert 'signal.alarm' not in src` and `assert 'Timer(' not in src` should also be deleted/relabeled or retained with justification.
findings.finding: AC2 specifies dropping only the `asyncio.run(...)` exact-string assertion from `test_no_total_runtime_timeout_mechanism`, but the test contains two additional brittle negative-space source-string assertions (`assert 'signal.alarm' not in src` and `assert 'Timer(' not in src`) that are equally fragile and equally non-behavioral. The plan is silent on their fate, leaving an implementer who reads AC2 literally to keep the two other assertions, resulting in an incomplete fix.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC2 to read: 'The no-total-timeout test drops all brittle source-string assertions (`signal.alarm`, `Timer(`, and the exact `asyncio.run(...)` string); if any negative-space guard is retained it must be explicitly labeled as a structural/doc guard and not counted as behavioral coverage.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC2's post-change end-state: state explicitly whether `test_no_total_runtime_timeout_mechanism` is deleted outright or retained with a specified weaker assertion (e.g. 'assert the function under test completes without raising'), and update AC2 to describe the observable outcome.
findings.finding: AC2 instructs the executor to 'drop the exact asyncio.run(...) string assertion' but does not specify what, if anything, should replace it — leaving the resulting test's content and scope undefined. 'The liveness property is anchored on the behavioral timeout probes' is a rationale, not a specification of the post-change assertion.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'The test is either deleted entirely OR retained with only the non-string-matching assertions (e.g. verifying no TimeoutError is raised); the `asyncio.run` substring check is removed and no substitute string-match is added.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or remove the 'coverage inventory' clause in AC3 with a reference to a concrete artifact (e.g., a specific doc file, a pytest marker, or a conftest `coverage_map` dict) that an implementer can actually update.
findings.finding: AC3 references 'any coverage inventory' as a concrete artifact, but no such file exists in the repo. The AC is unverifiable as written because there is no inventory document to update or check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Neither is counted as behavioral coverage in any coverage inventory.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
